name: BMAD-METHOD
description: Multi-agent agile AI development framework that prescribes distinct agent personas for each workflow phase.

description.value: Full-lifecycle multi-agent agile framework that drives AI coding agents through Analysis → Planning → Solutioning → Implementation phases, with 6+ specialized personas (Analyst, PM, Architect, Developer, UX Designer, Tech Writer) and a Party Mode that brings them into one conversation.

first-release.comment: Repo created 2025-04-13 per GitHub API; v6.x is the current line.

latest-release.comment: v6.3.0 published 2026-04-10 (verified via gh API on 2026-04-21).

license.comment: MIT per LICENSE file and marketplace.json. GitHub API classifies as 'NOASSERTION' because the LICENSE bundles a trademark notice; the grant itself is standard MIT.

pricing-model.comment: README explicitly states no paywalls or gated content.

covered-phases.comment: Analysis phase → discovery (brainstorming-report, product-brief); Planning → spec (PRD.md, ux-spec.md); Solutioning → architecture (architecture.md) and tasks (epics/stories); Implementation → implementation, review (code review + adversarial review), testing (QA test generation + TEA module), and retrospective notes.

methodology-style.comment: Genuinely multi-agent via distinct personas (PM, Architect, Dev, UX, Analyst, Tech Writer) and Party Mode. Agile in framing. Phase ordering and handoffs are the core value (process-centric). Plan (PRD/architecture) precedes execute (implementation).

opinionated.comment: Prescribes a named phase sequence with mandated artifacts per phase. A quick-flow exists for small work, but the default workflow is strictly structured.

ceremony-level.comment: Multiple mandated artifacts (brainstorming-report, product-brief, PRD, ux-spec, architecture, epics, stories, sprint-status), dedicated personas per phase, and an implementation-readiness gate push ceremony above 'medium'.

mechanization-level.comment: Installer scaffolds skills into the agent's IDE, and each workflow is a callable skill — but the user drives phase progression workflow-by-workflow rather than a single end-to-end run.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Explicit bmad-check-implementation-readiness gate (PASS/CONCERNS/FAIL) before phase 4; phase handoffs surface artifacts for human review.

approval-gates.comment: PRD (spec) and architecture (plan) are human-reviewed before moving on; epics/stories (tasks) are reviewed before sprint execution; readiness gate explicitly precedes implementation.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Default flow mandates human review at phase handoffs and the readiness gate. Community projects (e.g. bmad-autonomous-development) wrap BMAD to run autonomously, but that's not the default configuration.

autonomy-level.comment: Phase-level approvals with explicit readiness gate; individual workflows (skills) run end-to-end within their phase.

rollback-support.comment: No built-in revert/undo mechanism documented; users rely on git branches.

supported-agents.comment: 26 platform codes registered (claude-code and cursor marked 'preferred'). 'other' covers platforms without a dedicated tag: antigravity, auggie, codebuddy, crush, gemini, iflow, junie, kilo, kiro, ona, pi, qoder, qwen, rovo-dev, trae.

editor-integrations.comment: Installer drops skill files into .claude/skills, .cursor/skills, etc.; actual UX is whatever the host agent provides. BMAD itself has no dedicated VS Code or JetBrains plugin.

model-agnostic.comment: Works with any supported agent, which in turn runs against any backing model.

mcp-support.comment: No first-party MCP server. Community MCP servers exist (Dali1789/bmad-mcp-server, cexll/bmad-mcp-server, mkellerman/bmad-mcp-server) and an official MCP addition was discussed for a future release as of 2026-04-21.

artifact-formats.comment: Most artifacts are markdown; sprint-status.yaml tracks sprint state; customization and module config use TOML (e.g., _bmad/custom/config.toml).

artifacts-covered.comment: PRD/ux-spec (specs), architecture.md (architecture), brainstorming-report/product-brief (plans), epics and stories (tasks), QA test generation (test-plans), Tech Writer persona output (user-docs). No dedicated release-notes or change-logs workflow.

project-layout.value: _bmad/ (installed modules and core files) plus _bmad-output/ (generated artifacts: project-context.md, PRD.md, architecture.md, sprint-status.yaml), with per-IDE skill directories such as .claude/skills/ or .cursor/skills/ created by the installer.

artifacts-in-git.comment: All artifacts live under the project checkout; no SaaS backend.

language-agnostic.comment: No language-specific assumptions; tech stack decided during Solutioning phase. Game Dev Studio (BMGD) extension is the one vertical variant.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Primary enforcement is persona/skill prompt text (instructional). Adversarial Review workflow is a second-agent verification (agent-review). Implementation readiness gate requires human PASS decision (manual-approval).

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Workflow is 'flexible but progressive' — the bmad-quick-dev workflow explicitly skips phases 1-3 for small, well-understood work. Phases inform one another but are not hard-gated in software.

artifact-validation.comment: Markdown artifacts follow templates but aren't schema-validated. sprint-status.yaml is structured but no validator is documented.

memory-implementation.comment: project-context.md (with YAML frontmatter) stored at _bmad-output/project-context.md is loaded by every implementation workflow; no vector DB or knowledge graph.

persistent-memory.comment: Artifacts and project-context.md are committed to the repo and loaded across sessions.

project-context-files.comment: Produces project-context.md; repo itself ships AGENTS.md and references CLAUDE.md-style always-rules files.

cost-tracking.comment: No built-in token or cost telemetry; the host agent handles any such reporting.

run-history.comment: sprint-status.yaml tracks sprint progress, but there is no framework-level structured run history or trace store.

telemetry-opt.comment: No telemetry collection disclosed or referenced; attribute not meaningfully applicable to a collection of prompt/skill files and a CLI installer.

custom-templates.comment: Three-layer override system (personal .user.toml, team .toml, skill defaults) lets users customize personas, principles, menu items, and activation steps while staying update-compatible.

hooks.comment: activation_steps_prepend and activation_steps_append give users pre/post hooks on agent activation; append-only arrays accumulate across layers.

sub-agents.comment: Users can define custom agents/personas and organizational expansion modules; BMad Builder (BMB) is the official tool for this.

marketplace.comment: v6.3.0 introduced a marketplace ecosystem (.claude-plugin/marketplace.json); BMad Builder and community modules extend the catalog.

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install is `npx bmad-method install`; requires Node.js 20+.

setup-time.comment: Single npx command scaffolds the project; non-interactive mode supported for CI/CD.

requires-account.comment: BMAD itself requires no login; the backing AI agent may have its own auth.

git-worktree-support.comment: Not explicitly supported; {project-root}/_bmad resolution is known to break in bare-repo worktree setups (issue #1738, open as of 2026-04-21).

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated docs site organized by Diátaxis (tutorials / how-to / explanation / reference), AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG, multi-language READMEs, and active blog coverage. Gaps: MCP story, telemetry, and rollback not covered.

active-maintenance.comment: Last push 2026-04-20; v6.3.0 released 2026-04-10 — well within 90 days.

example-projects.comment: README links to YouTube walkthroughs and community case studies; Vibe Sparking AI publishes detailed release-by-release walkthroughs; community Discord shares project examples.

community-channel.comment: Discord is the primary community hub per README; GitHub Discussions also active. YouTube and X/Twitter are promoted but aren't discussion channels.
